metadata.title: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
metadata.study_design: Intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate during prolonged overnight fast (12-19h) in healthy, lean young men and women
metadata.demographics: Healthy, lean young men and women (n=12), matched for age and BMI, free from disease, weight stable, not taking lipid-lowering medication

evidence.quote: British Journal of Nutrition (2011), 105, 857–865
Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
Kyriakoula Marinou1,2, Martin Adiels3, Leanne Hodson1, Keith N. Frayn1, Fredrik Karpe1 and Barbara A. Fielding1*
Volunteers were studied during a prolonged overnight fast (12–19h) using an intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate.
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied, and matched for age and BMI with no significant difference in waist circumference
in exact accordance with the six men and six women in the present study

methods_participants.label: Inclusion Criteria - Health Status
methods_participants.value: Healthy, lean, weight stable, no disease, no lipid-lowering medication

evidence.quote: Subjects, recruited from the wider Oxford community via advertisement, were free from any disease, weight stable and not taking any lipid-lowering medication or medication that would alter lipid metabolism.

methods_participants.label: Pre-study Protocol - Exercise and Alcohol Avoidance

evidence.quote: Before the study day, subjects were asked to avoid foodstuffs naturally enriched in 13C for 48h
Fat mass was measured by bioelectrical impedance analysis, and fat-free mass was calculated as the difference between total body weight and fat mass.

At time 0, a continuous intravenous infusion (0·04 mmol/kg per min) of potassium [U-13C]palmitate (isotope purity 98%; Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, Inc., Andover, MA, USA) complexed to human albumin was started

methods_participants.label: Blood Sampling - Baseline
methods_participants.value: Baseline blood sample for background isotopic enrichment

evidence.quote: A cannula was inserted into an antecubital vein, and a baseline blood sample was taken for background isotopic enrichment.
plasma NEFA, VLDL-TAG and 3-OHB concentrations were determined enzymatically

methods_participants.label: VLDL Isolation - Centrifugation Conditions 1
methods_participants.method: Sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

evidence.quote: In ten subjects, VLDL (Svedberg flotation rate (Sf) 60–400) and VLDL (Sf 20–60) were isolated by sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

methods_participants.label: 3-OHB Isotopic Enrichment - Method
methods_participants.value: Modified method of Beylot et al.

evidence.quote: Isotopic enrichment from [U-13C]fatty acids appearing in 3-OHB in deproteinised plasma was measured using a modified method of Beylot et al.
The studies were performed during the first week (follicular phase) of the menstrual cycle

methods_participants.label: Ethical Approval
methods_participants.value: Oxford Shire Clinical Research Ethics Committee

evidence.quote: All procedures involving human subjects were approved by the Oxfordshire Clinical Research Ethics Committee. Written informed consent was obtained from all subjects.

narrative_results.statement: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

evidence.quote: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

narrative_results.statement: The transfer of 13C from palmitate to plasma 3-OHB reached a plateau 6-7 hours after infusion start in women but was still increasing at 6 hours in men

evidence.quote: the transfer of 13C from [U-13C]palmitate to plasma [13C]3-OHB reached a plateau 6–7 h after the start of the infusion in women but was still increasing at 6 h in men

narrative_results.statement: In women, systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production, but this was not observed in men

evidence.quote: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production. However, in men, this was not observed during the course of the study (P<0·01)

narrative_results.statement: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL-TAG

evidence.quote: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL - or VLDL -TAG
